revision.comment: Bot: Creating normal ill redirect for [[Category:Lua-based templates]].
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:Template:R from CamelCase]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:qq]]
#redirect[[:en:Template:R from CamelCase]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects R]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects R]]" to " <noinclude>[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects R]]</noinclude>"
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:Template:R from CamelCase]] <noinclude>[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects R]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia template redirects R

revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}} </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Documentation}}" to "{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]]"
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23530060143|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: <noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] </noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

<noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude> <!-- Place categories on /doc subpage; interwikis go to Wikidata. --> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24957526818|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<noinclude>{{This is a redirect/rcat}} </noinclude>{{Redirect template |id=R from CamelCase |name=From a camel case title |from=a camel case page name |info=In the initial versions of Wikipedia, all links had to be "[[:en:CamelCase|CamelCase]]", i.e., words that used medial capitals; they are "two-humped" like a [[:en:Bactrian camel|Bactrian camel]]. These are kept as redirects to maintain edit history and to avoid breaking links that may have been made externally. |all category=Redirects with old history |printworthy=no }}<noinclude>
{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]]
[[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
